system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 92.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: -. Scope: Self only; Permanent. Progression: +1 Armour Class/Rank.

Rules Summary: Raises the character's Armour Class by making them harder to hit, damage, or target.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: Constitution. Scope: Self only; Ongoing. Progression: 1 Alternate Identity/Rank.

Rules Summary: Gives the character alternate outward identities without changing their underlying statistics.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: -. Scope: Self only; Permanent. Progression: +1 Armour Proficiency/Rank.

Rules Summary: Removes the penalties for using a selected armour category.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: -. Scope: Self only; Permanent. Progression: +1 Ability Score/Rank.

Attribute Cost: 2 Points/Rank. Relevant Ability: Intelligence. Scope: Self only; Instantaneous. Progression: Descriptive.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: -. Scope: Self only; Permanent. Progression: +1 Attack Bonus/Rank.

system.trackingNotes: Construct the companion from additional Points by Rank.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 98.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: Wisdom. Scope: 10-foot radius; 1 minute. Progression: 1 Environment/Rank.

Attribute Cost: 3 Points/Rank. Relevant Ability: Constitution. Scope: Self only; Ongoing. Progression: Temporarily gain +1 Point/Rank for every converted point source.

system.description:

Rules Summary: Creates a significant personal item or piece of gear built from Points.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: Dexterity. Scope: Self only; Ongoing. Progression: Descriptive.

Rules Summary: Increases the distance the character can leap.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: Intelligence. Scope: Self only; Ongoing. Progression: 1 Language/Rank.

Rules Summary: Adds fluency in one additional language per Rank.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 106.

Rules Summary: Raises damage output for a broad class of physical attacks.

Rules Summary: Raises damage output for a narrower attack category.

Attribute Cost: 4 Points/Rank. Relevant Ability: Intelligence. Scope: Self only; Must touch character; 1 minute. Progression: Can imitate Attributes up to a maximum Rank.

system.trackingNotes: Copied Attributes remain limited by this Attribute's Rank.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 106.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 108.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 108.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: Charisma or Intelligence. Scope: Situational use; Ongoing. Progression: 1 Training Technique/Rank.

Rules Summary: Adds techniques for commanding, coordinating, or improving trained monsters.
system.energyCost: Technique-dependent; Inspire Monster costs 5 Energy/round, Instil techniques cost 10 Energy each
system.trackingMode: Track selected training techniques, linked monster, command, benefit, Energy cost, and duration.
system.trainingTechniques:
Analyse Monster: identify species; advantage on Intelligence checks for rare/evolved monsters; judge point total, HP, and elemental association.
Battle Motto: once per dramatic scene; enemy trainer has disadvantage on first two Initiative rolls while the trainer has advantage on their first two.
Breed Monster: DC 15 Charisma check to produce a viable hybrid from two monsters that do not normally mate.
Feed Monster: supervised nutrition up to half-hour per creature per day; prevents disease and doubles lost Hit Point recovery.
Inspire Monster: free action at the start of a combat round; spend 5 Energy to give +2 attack rolls, +2 Armour Class, or restore 1d8 HP for the round.
Instil Discipline: one-hour session; trainer and monster spend 10 Energy each; lasts one session/adventuring day and grants +2 Armour Class plus controlled behaviour.
Instil Ferocity: one-hour session; trainer and monster spend 10 Energy each; lasts one session/adventuring day and grants +2 attack rolls, or +4 against a specific trained target.
Monster Tactics: when supervising and able to shout orders, monsters gain advantage on Initiative rolls through the battle.
Nurse Monster: under care, monster heals HP three times faster; five times faster if the trainer also has Feed Monster.
Stat Master: encyclopaedic knowledge of trainers, teams, and monster dojos; DM may require a DC 15 Intelligence check.
system.trackingNotes: Only characters with Companion or Minions usually assign Monster Training. Each Rank grants one training technique.

system.description:

system.duration: Spell-dependent; once per long rest unless cantrip
system.energyCost: Rank squared Energy; cantrips can be used at will
system.trackingMode: Link a spell item or store the spell-like effect, spell level, prerequisites, usage, and Energy cost.
system.spellPrerequisites: Recommended character Level prerequisite equals the spell Level, twice the spell Level, or another DM-approved requirement; Race-granted Spell-Like Abilities may ignore this.
system.spellUsage: Once per long rest after use; cantrips can be used at will.
system.trackingNotes: Rank 1 is equivalent to a cantrip, Rank 2 to a 1st-Level spell, up through Rank 10 as a 9th-Level spell.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 118.

system.weaponNotes: Base damage is 1d4 per Rank before Enhancements or Limiters.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Attribute Cost: 1 Point/Rank. Relevant Ability: Variable. Scope: Self only; Permanent. Progression: Descriptive.

Rules Summary: Adds proficiency with selected weapon groups or specific weapons.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 143.

Category: Attribute Enhancement. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 0-6 assignments.

Rules Summary: Expands an Attribute into a radius of effect, with larger areas requiring more assignments.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 143.

Category: Attribute Enhancement. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 0-10 assignments.

Rules Summary: Extends how long an Attribute's effect remains active after use, from minutes up to permanent effects with DM approval.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 143.

Category: Attribute Enhancement. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 0-3 assignments.

Rules Summary: Lets a Rank 1 Attribute keep a lower effective Rank after Limiters would otherwise raise its effect too far.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 143.

Category: Attribute Enhancement. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 0-6 assignments.

Rules Summary: Lets an Attribute originate away from the character, from touch range to city-scale distances.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 143.

Category: Attribute Enhancement. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 0-6 assignments.

Rules Summary: Allows an Attribute to affect multiple people or objects at the same time.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 145.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Requires preparation before the Attribute can be used, ranging from one round to one hour.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 145.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Requires one or more special assistants to be present for the Attribute to activate or continue.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 145.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Creates a harmful side effect when the Attribute fails or misses by the specified margin.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 145.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Limits the Attribute to a small number of uses each day or session.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 146.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Requires ongoing focus to keep a non-instant Attribute active.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 146.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Consumes a special focus, reagent, or offering whenever the Attribute is used.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 146.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Prevents the Attribute from taking effect until one or more other Attributes are active.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 146.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Spends Energy to activate or maintain the Attribute when optional Energy rules are used.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 146.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Makes the Attribute's use noticeable or traceable by specified detection methods.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 146.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Restricts use to moments of significant, strong, or extreme emotional investment.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 147.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Limits the Attribute to a specific setting, condition, or situational trigger.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 147.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Requires tools, furnishings, materials, or accessories that aid the Attribute without embodying it.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 147.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Lets the character grant the Attribute's use to others for a dramatic scene, usually with Charges.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 147.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Makes transformation-style Attributes difficult or slow to undo.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 148.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Forces the Attribute to function at maximum effective strength instead of allowing lower output.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 148.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Limits an Item-contained Attribute so it benefits the object more than its user.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 148.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Keeps the Attribute always active when that constancy is inconvenient.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 148.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Requires a rest period after using an ongoing Attribute before it can function again.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 148.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Keeps the Attribute normally active but allows temporary suppression by spending Energy.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 148.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Covers custom restrictions not otherwise listed, with severity set by player and DM agreement.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 148.

Category: Attribute Limiter. Applies To: Attribute. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Requires an Ability check or creates unreliable results when the Attribute is used.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 125.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Grants advantage on attack rolls with the Weapon.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 125.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-4.

Rules Summary: Turns the Weapon into an area attack with larger radii at higher assignments.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 125.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Automatically affects creatures that touch the user rather than requiring a normal attack action.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 125.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Can greatly increase or reduce damage based on a Constitution save.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 125.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-2.

Rules Summary: Lets the Weapon deliver its effect through skin contact.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 126.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Passes the Weapon's damage or effects to others who touch the affected target unless resisted.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 126.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1+.

Rules Summary: Adds lingering damage over rounds, hours, or days after the initial successful attack.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 126.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Reduces a specified Ability Score in addition to any damage.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 126.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1+.

Rules Summary: Drains Energy from the target in addition to any damage.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Overloads a sense, such as sight or hearing, unless the target resists.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Allows a whip-like or extendible attack to trip or disarm instead of dealing damage.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Lets a narrowly missed projectile or burst return for one more attempt next round.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 2 or 4.

Rules Summary: Can immobilise a target with effects such as sleep, paralysis, or petrification.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 3.

Rules Summary: Hides the attack's visible origin and may enable surprise.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Makes wounds harder or impossible to heal until special conditions are met.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Allows ranged attacks to arc over cover when the target's location is known.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Distracts or partially blinds the target, imposing disadvantage if they fail to resist.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Attaches this Weapon to a master Weapon so it hits automatically when the master attack succeeds.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 127.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Allows the Weapon to affect targets across dimensional or incorporeal boundaries.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-2.

Rules Summary: Reduces the Armour Class benefit of physical armour or hide.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Creates a linear ground fissure or shock wave under a target.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-4.

Rules Summary: Extends a Weapon beyond melee reach, from 10 feet to 1,000 feet.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Extends melee reach and can let the wielder strike first against shorter melee weapons.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Lets an Area, Aura, or Spreading Weapon choose who is affected.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1+.

Rules Summary: Lets the attack spread to additional nearby targets in a line, cone, or spray.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1+.

Rules Summary: Restrains targets in physical entanglements that must be damaged or broken.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Doubles damage and selected effects against a chosen individual, group, or common category.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 128.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Places the Weapon as a waiting trap that triggers later.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 129.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1+.

Rules Summary: Covers a custom Weapon benefit approved and priced by the DM.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 129.

Category: Weapon Enhancement. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 2 or 4.

Rules Summary: Transfers lost Hit Points, Energy, or Ability values to the attacker for a short time.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 129.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Limits the Weapon to a small amount of carried ammunition before it must be replenished.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1-3.

Rules Summary: Restricts the Weapon to a specified category, group, or individual target.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Requires two appropriately sized hands or appendages to use the Weapon properly.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Imposes disadvantage on attack rolls with the Weapon.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Requires the Weapon's substance to be swallowed before it affects the target.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Makes the Weapon poor against armour or heavy clothing, increasing the defender's AC.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 2 or 4.

Rules Summary: Replaces the attack roll with a target Constitution or Dexterity save.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Only damages living targets and can sometimes be countered by simple precautions.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1+.

Rules Summary: Covers a custom Weapon restriction approved and priced by the DM.

Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 130.

Category: Weapon Limiter. Applies To: Weapon. Assignments: 1.

Rules Summary: Makes the Weapon fail or malfunction on a natural 1 until a condition is met.
